title: Why Schools Must Teach Financial Literacy
subtitle: 大学生为何对理财一无所知

paragraphs.en: As a university student, I've come to realize just how little I know about money. I've come to the brutal realization that Australia's youth are being done a great disservice by not receiving any consistent financial education. Diving headfirst into the crash course of starting university, I've quickly tried to get myself up to speed with the financial terms of the modern era to help manage my personal finances.
paragraphs.zh: 作为一名大学生，我逐渐意识到自己对金钱的了解何其匮乏。我残酷地认识到，澳大利亚年轻人因未接受持续的金融教育而深受其害。一头扎进大学的速成课程后，我迅速努力让自己跟上现代金融术语，以便管理个人财务。

words.meaning: adj. 残酷的，令人难受的

paragraphs.en: I've read some financial education books, done some online learning, and have spoken to my nearest and dearest for their pearls of financial wisdom. There are undoubtedly hundreds of great resources out there for those wishing to improve their financial literacy, but while I was researching, I still kept wishing that I didn't have to play catch-up with the money world. I felt that I was sailing the financial seas with no skills and no life jacket!
paragraphs.zh: 我读过一些金融教育书籍，进行过在线学习，还向最亲近的人请教过他们的金融智慧。对于想提高金融素养的人来说，无疑有数百种优质资源可用，但我在研究时仍一直希望自己不必在金钱世界里拼命追赶。我感觉自己就像在金融海洋中航行，既无技能也无救生衣！

paragraphs.en: However, after talking to my friends who felt the same, I quickly realized that it wasn't my ignorance but the lack of financial education in our schooling system that is leaving high schoolers seriously behind in the modern world of money. Let's compare a theoretical financial education subject to the standard compulsory English class. On average, English may not be the most popular subject, but it's consistently on the schedule throughout high school, with all students graduating fluent in English.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，与有同感的朋友交谈后，我很快意识到并非我无知，而是教育体系中金融教育的缺失让高中生在当今金钱世界中严重落后。让我们将一门理论上的金融教育科目与标准的必修英语课做个比较。平均而言，英语或许不是最受欢迎的科目，但它始终贯穿整个高中课程，所有学生毕业时都能流利使用英语。

paragraphs.en: A financial education subject should do just the same. It shouldn't be just a one-day event but a course integrated throughout the whole of high school that would allow students to gradually expand their financial literacy, and would prevent the need for a 'catch up' phase once we're out on our own after graduation. In the same way that learning a language or new skills takes time, building financial skills requires practice and years to gradually accumulate bits and pieces of knowledge.
paragraphs.zh: 金融教育科目也应如此。它不应只是一日活动，而应是一门贯穿整个高中的课程，让学生逐步扩展金融素养，避免毕业后独自闯荡时还需要“追赶”。正如学习语言或新技能需要时间一样，培养财务技能也需要练习和多年时间逐步积累点滴知识。

words.meaning: adj. 整合的，融为一体的

paragraphs.en: Giving young people the opportunity to become familiar with the world of money would provide them with a great advantage to enter adulthood with confidence and security so that they are able to manage their own money and look after themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 给予年轻人熟悉金钱世界的机会，将为他们提供巨大优势，让他们能自信、安心地步入成年，从而有能力管理自己的财务并照顾好自己。
